title: Habenaria macrorchis
taxonomy: Habenaria macrorchis is a species of orchid in the genus Habenaria. It belongs to the family Orchidaceae, commonly known as the orchid family. Within this family, Habenaria macrorchis is classified under the subfamily Orchidoideae. This subfamily further categorizes the species into the tribe Orchideae, and specifically, the subtribe Orchidinae. The exact classification of Habenaria macrorchis may be subject to further study and refinement.

intro: Habenaria macrorchis, also known as Himalayan orchid or Pachmo, is a type of orchid plant that belongs to the family Orchidaceae. It is traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Habenaria macrorchis include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Habenaria macrorchis is commonly prepared as a decoction, powder, or infusion, and is also used in combination with other herbs to create various herbal preparations, including Ayurvedic tonics and polyherbal formulations.
